title: Rambling about Yui the anti universe deicides and plug depths
display_title: Rambling about Yui the anti universe deicides and plug depths
wikitext:
[]: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Fri Sep 03, 2021 11:03 pm I don't know how to sum this up neatly, hence the title. So, I've just finished watching 3.0+1.0 again and a few lines stick out to me. The first is when Gendo says that Yui had been to the Golgotha Object. The second is Shinji mentioning that Gendo's wish is to "see Mother off" which is the "god-slaying." This is a tough little knot to puzzle out, and something that's been bothering me since watching the film for the first time. My initial thought is: did Gendo think Yui was trapped in the anti-universe? It might sound crazy, but bear with me. We know Yui vanished entirely during her experiment with the core of Unit 01 in the Rebuild continuity. We know that the Rebuilds have downplayed the existence of Yui's soul/consciousness within Unit 01. Fuyutsuki said in 3.33 something along the lines of that the only thing left of Yui was the information that became the Evangelion control system and Rei (so, computer code and genetic data.) They inserted Yui into the core and she vanished. What if the cores link to the anti-universe in some way? Plug depth does bizarre things to pilots and results in awakened Evangelions. Shinji, for example, vanished entirely at the climax of 2.22 until he was 'salvaged' by WILLE fourteen years later -- but the process resulted in him being the third entity. Was WILLE basically 'fishing' through the anti-universe for Shinji? So, by being inserted into Unit 01's core, did Yui 'vanish' into the anti-universe and become trapped? Was Gendo's plan to reunite with Yui and save her or, alternatively, 'kill' what was left of her so she might go to her final rest? Has anyone tried to figure out these lines?

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sat Sep 04, 2021 12:03 am Gendo's Glasses wrote: We know that the Rebuilds have downplayed the existence of Yui's soul/consciousness within Unit 01.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by pir2confusion » Sat Sep 04, 2021 4:36 am I agree that from what we are told Yui was in the Golgotha object but whatever Yui did was part of her plan so I don't think she was trapped or anything like that. Also the implication of Yui being in the Golgotha object is that she is or was god. Gendo's plan is definitely to be together with Yui, though I don't really understand if it is to slay god Yui so they both die together or that they are together and now both gods For some less thought out rambling of what those plans are, if the series are completely separate what does being in the Golgotha object allow someone or thing to do. What is an impact vs a neon genesis? Are impacts a rewriting of a world and neon genesis being a creation of a new one or just a change to an existing place? If the series are linked and then with some fun word play maybe Yui became a god like being in EOE. Yui's plan was the new world of evangelion. The agreement with Seele was trying to make it so all the souls are transformed into evangelion. While the rebuild series is the creation of a new world of (not)evangelion.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by T. K. Simon » Sat Sep 04, 2021 7:16 am In the movie is it implicit that Shinji is the one who has been rewinding time during the instrumentality (in each cycle), in the Neon Genesis scene? If so, I highly doubt that Shinji rebooted the world a little after eoe. Unless a divine being executes it. Possibly it would have been the earlier versions of Shinji RoE. Or is it a unique property in the rebuilds Gendo has the key in his power, which according to him allows him to see beyond his reality, allowing him to see his "memories" (which can be from each existing cycle, or his self of different dimensions) The golgota object creates places based on people's memories, in this case, Shinji, allows him to see his self from other cycles or dimensions and his before memories. The kaworu coffins (with seele rebuild logo) exist in 1.0, they seem to have a creation function for kaworu, which is aware of other cycles, but if the golgota object creates based on memories, why does everyone have the logo from seele rebuild? Unless kaworu every time he dies, he goes to another cycle, which although it is unlikely, is that one cycle does not end when another begins. Or the world reboots during instrumentality (at the end of it), which is also unlikely in EoE because in the movie, Shinji rejects instrumentality, a decision that affects all humanity, and it seems that Shinji has been on the beach long enough to lose the hope (his eyes), a month or about 3 weeks.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by deepseawaterport » Sat Sep 04, 2021 8:03 am The crazy thing for me is that Yui in the NTE is either a villain or she doesn't make sense at all. She oversaw and knew all the nerv projects, such as having her son psychologically stunted and having two clone girls programmed to feel attracted to him. She visited this anti universe space and she willingly submerged herself in Eva 01. In EoE it seems that her agenda was to be what Willie was in 3.0+1.0, this ark carrying the souls and remains of earth/humanity across space. But now she doesn't have any agenda at all, it really doesn't make sense, why she would let Shinji erase the Evas, which is her life time work, the work she sacrificed herself to? Why would she make her son depressed and insecure on purpose?

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Sep 04, 2021 8:49 am deepseawaterport wrote: The crazy thing for me is that Yui in the NTE is either a villain or she doesn't make sense at all. She oversaw and knew all the nerv projects, such as having her son psychologically stunted and having two clone girls programmed to feel attracted to him. She visited this anti universe space and she willingly submerged herself in Eva 01. In EoE it seems that her agenda was to be what Willie was in 3.0+1.0, this ark carrying the souls and remains of earth/humanity across space. But now she doesn't have any agenda at all, it really doesn't make sense, why she would let Shinji erase the Evas, which is her life time work, the work she sacrificed herself to? Why would she make her son depressed and insecure on purpose?

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Sat Sep 04, 2021 11:02 am I'd caution people against ascribing anything to Yui and her motivations based on the original series as far as it concerns the Rebuild. Yui in the original series is an odd character, someone who we see only through the eyes of the most biased individuals involved, who bears a fair amount of capability for how things turned out on her shoulders ("Time to get sucked into Unit 01 and not tell my husband, hope he takes it well!") In the Rebuilds, we don't really know anything about Yui and we get told even less. I'm not really convinced that Yui would've known anything nor been a member of the privileged few as she was in the original. She's undeniably very talented, but I really do feel like she just so happened to be the first 'test pilot' and vanished as a consequence.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Archer » Sat Sep 04, 2021 12:23 pm I kind of like the idea of cores being gateways to the anti-universe. Possibly, the NTE equivalent to the FAR (and this everything to do with Evas and angels) originated from the anti-universe to begin with.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Sun Sep 05, 2021 8:06 pm A pal of mine had the theory that the 'gods' Gendo speaks of are simply us. After all, the anti-universe is the place where fiction and reality are blurred. And isn't Evangelion just fiction from our perspective? And people sure do love isekai these days.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sun Sep 05, 2021 9:28 pm Upon watching the scene where Shniji asks Gendo what he wishes for, Gendo replied he's wants to accept the reality that Shinji had previously rejected. Like Kaworu, does Gendo know of the prior cycles too?

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Sun Sep 05, 2021 9:36 pm Perhaps not like Kaworu does, but yes, Gendo does appear to glean beyond the veil and understand that there was an alternative reality where Shinji rejected Instrumentality. However, I don't view that as being a part of the 'cycle' as such, and more that it links to what Gendo says how there's no difference between fiction and reality in that space.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sun Sep 05, 2021 10:24 pm Near the end of the film, Shinji says something like: I finally understand what Dad's God slaying really meant: he just wanted to send mom off.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by Gendo's Glasses » Mon Sep 06, 2021 7:12 pm I really like that reading, DantesInferno ! Thanks for posting it. It's definitely helpful with assembling the jigsaw pieces. It's a shamer they went with pseudo evolution -- pseudo deification makes so much more sense.

[]: Re: Rambling about Yui, the anti-universe, deicides, and plug depths...             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Tue Sep 07, 2021 6:44 am Gendo's Glasses wrote: I really like that reading, DantesInferno ! Thanks for posting it. It's definitely helpful with assembling the jigsaw pieces. It's a shamer they went with pseudo evolution -- pseudo deification makes so much more sense.
